implementation.fast_path: In coalesced Patch mode, a complete simple unqualified <is><t>...</t></is> payload already present in the bounded reader window is emitted as one exact-byte span.
implementation.fallback: Rich text, phonetic or extension markup, namespace aliases, boundary-split payloads and malformed candidates retain the ordinary parser path and diagnostics.
implementation.formula_boundary: Formula <f> markup remains visible to the transformer and is never consumed by the inline-string fast path.

interpretation.supported_claim: On this machine and recorded one-million-cell mixed-inline level-1 targeted-upsert workload, the simple inline-string fast path reduced parser, source-callback and transform-action traffic by 22.73%, 17.24% and 31.23%; transform median fell by 21.10% while median process peak working set changed by about 0.004 MB.
interpretation.compatibility: Focused formula/hyperlink tests passed, the formula guard profile reported zero inline fast-path traffic, and representative mixed-inline/formula outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 while preserving the recorded external hyperlink target.
interpretation.next_hotspot: After parser/action wrapper reduction, the next lane is a separate level-6 compression backend/parameter profile; no backend change is justified by this bundle.

scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one-million-cell 10-column mixed-inline and formula distributions, 1000 targeted upserts, source DEFLATE level 6 and output DEFLATE level 1. No cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, Office, backend-comparison or OpenXLSX superiority claim.
